name: industrial_policy_licensing_backlog_northpine_ind_col
premise: A Industrial Policy response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving industrial policy licensing backlog northpine ind col. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the press briefing. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as individual vs collective under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability.

agents.name: Leila Vasquez
agents.identity: I am Leila Vasquez, accountable for a defensible call in Industrial Policy when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays collective for individual.

agents.name: Talia Okoye
agents.identity: I am Talia Okoye, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Industrial Policy.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so collective isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of individual.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect collective.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to collective to secure individual under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the press briefing. Evidence is partial. Leila Vasquez must decide under individual vs collective. What does Leila Vasquez do?
